metadata.description: SmartPatch Registry Controls — production-grade schema (REG-01 .. REG-06). Each control contains enforcement commands, validation checks, failure modes, privileges and authoritative references. Added sensible registry controls (Remote Registry disable, RestrictAnonymous) based on scope/criteria.
metadata.author: SmartPatch Team (converted)

controls.name: Disable AutoRun / AutoPlay (NoDriveTypeAutoRun)
controls.description: Disable automatic execution from removable and other drives by configuring NoDriveTypeAutoRun to an appropriate value via policy.
controls.rationale: AutoRun/AutoPlay can automatically execute malicious content from removable media. Disabling prevents malware propagation via Autorun.inf and reduces attack surface.

os_applicability.notes: Behavior and default values vary by OS; prefer GPO settings which set NoDriveTypeAutoRun appropriately.

commands: Configure 'Turn off Autoplay' Computer Configuration → Administrative Templates → Windows Components → AutoPlay Policies → 'Turn off Autoplay' = Enabled (All drives)
This results in NoDriveTypeAutoRun = 0xFF (255) on managed systems.
enforcement_methods.notes: Use GPO to ensure consistent value. GPO sets NoDriveTypeAutoRun to 255 for 'Disable on all drives'.
0xFF (255) disables AutoPlay on all drives. Evaluate exceptions and test; Microsoft/CISA note behavioral caveats with some OS versions.

validation_checks.success_criteria: NoDriveTypeAutoRun exists and equals 0xFF (255) or the configured baseline value.

validation_checks.command: Insert removable media in a test host and verify no AutoRun actions execute; verify AutoPlay UI behavior per policy.
validation_checks.success_criteria: No automatic execution; only manual launch allowed or behavior matches selected policy.

failure_modes: Some hardware (HotStart buttons, specialized kiosks) may stop working if Autoplay fully disabled; test hardware-dependent devices.
CISA / historical OS caveats: certain OS builds may still behave unexpectedly with NoDriveTypeAutoRun—test on representative images.
controls.functional_impact: Low–Medium (depends on workflows that rely on AutoPlay).

priority.priority_reason: Reduces a common propagation vector for removable-media malware.

audit_query_examples: Insert test USB and observe (or run controlled lab test)
controls.implementation_notes: Deploy via GPO; pilot on representative hardware (laptops, kiosks, lab devices). Document exceptions and rationale for any non-255 values.

controls.name: Block AlwaysInstallElevated (MSI elevation misconfiguration)
controls.description: Ensure AlwaysInstallElevated is disabled in both HKLM and HKCU to prevent non-admin users from installing MSI packages with SYSTEM privileges.
controls.rationale: When AlwaysInstallElevated = 1 in both machine and user hives, attackers can escalate privileges by providing a crafted MSI. Baselines require both keys be 0 (or unset).

os_applicability.notes: Applies to MSI installer behavior across supported Windows versions.

enforcement_methods.notes: Setting to 0 disables the AlwaysInstallElevated privilege. If GPO manages this, enforce via GPO Administrative Templates.

enforcement_methods.description: Enforce via relevant MSI/Installer administrative templates or deploy registry enforcement to both hives for all users.

validation_checks.command: reg query "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Installer" /v AlwaysInstallElevated & reg query "HKCU\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Installer" /v AlwaysInstallElevated
validation_checks.success_criteria: Both keys exist and equal 0 (or keys absent / GPO enforces policy that results in 0).

validation_checks.command: Attempt to install an MSI as a non-admin in an isolated lab environment to confirm no elevation occurs.
validation_checks.success_criteria: Non-admin MSI installation does not gain SYSTEM privileges.

failure_modes: If set to 0 incorrectly via custom MSI tooling that expects elevation, some automated installs may fail; coordinate with application owners.
If HKCU is not set for all users by policy, user-scope misconfigurations remain a risk.
controls.functional_impact: Low (standard secure practice); may require procedures for legitimate automated installs.

priority.priority_reason: Mitigates a proven privilege-escalation misconfiguration (widely exploited in real-world attacks).

references.title: SpecterOps / community checks for AlwaysInstallElevated misconfig

controls.implementation_notes: Enforce via GPO where possible; verify HKCU for user accounts and include in compliance reporting. Remediate exceptions with documented and auditable installer processes.

controls.name: Restrict administrative shares (AutoShareWks / AutoShareServer)
controls.description: Control automatic creation of default administrative shares (C$, ADMIN$, etc.) by setting AutoShareWks (workstations) or AutoShareServer (servers) where business-justified and after risk assessment.
controls.rationale: Administrative shares facilitate remote management but also increase attack surface; some environments require disabling them on endpoints where remote admin is not needed.

os_applicability.notes: Server-specific guidance differs; use AutoShareServer on servers and AutoShareWks on workstations.

commands: Restart Server service: net stop server & net start server  (or reboot)
enforcement_methods.notes: Setting AutoShareWks/AutoShareServer to 0 prevents automatic creation of default admin shares (IPC$ and manually created shares are unaffected per MS guidance).

validation_checks.command: reg query "HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\LanmanServer\Parameters" /v AutoShareWks & reg query "HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\LanmanServer\Parameters" /v AutoShareServer
validation_checks.success_criteria: AutoShareWks/AutoShareServer exist and equal 0 on systems where admin shares are to be disabled.
Administrative shares (C$, ADMIN$) absent after service restart on systems configured to disable creation.

failure_modes: Disabling admin shares can break remote management/backup/monitoring workflows that rely on these shares — validate dependencies before change.
Some groups recommend leaving admin shares for centralised management and instead controlling access with ACLs and network segmentation.
controls.functional_impact: Medium — impacts remote admin tooling and backup/management that use admin shares.

priority.priority_reason: Reduces a reconnaissance/admin vector but may impact operations; treat as optional per asset class.

references.title: How to remove administrative shares in Windows — Microsoft Learn

controls.implementation_notes: Apply selectively after inventorying tools that rely on admin shares (SCCM, backups, remote management). Provide alternative management paths before disabling on critical systems.

controls.name: Disable insecure guest SMB authentication (AllowInsecureGuestAuth = 0)
controls.description: Disable insecure guest authentication for SMB clients to prevent unauthenticated anonymous SMB access to file shares.
controls.rationale: Insecure guest auth allows unauthenticated access to file shares (common on consumer NAS). Enterprises should disable it to require authenticated access and reduce anonymous exposure.

os_applicability.notes: Windows file servers do not use insecure guest auth by default; consumer devices might.

enforcement_methods.notes: Set AllowInsecureGuestAuth = 0 to prevent insecure guest logons on SMB client. For server-side guest restrictions, consider share permissions and server configuration.

enforcement_methods.description: Manage via GPO or server role settings where appropriate; do not enable insecure guest logons unless absolutely required and documented.

validation_checks.command: Attempt guest (unauthenticated) SMB access to a share from a test client; verify access denied.

failure_modes: Consumer/legacy NAS devices relying on guest auth will fail to mount shares — document exceptions and isolate or replace devices.
Incorrect server-side configuration may still permit anonymous access via misconfigured share ACLs; verify server ACLs.
controls.functional_impact: Low–Medium depending on presence of guest-dependent devices.

priority.priority_reason: Prevents unauthenticated file access and aligns with secure-by-default posture.

audit_query_examples: Attempt SMB guest connection in a lab

references.title: Guidance: guest access in SMB2/SMB3 is disabled by default — Microsoft

controls.implementation_notes: Inventory consumer NAS and other devices; provide exception/compensating controls (network segmentation, authenticated proxy) for legacy devices.

controls.name: Disable Remote Registry service (Start = 4)
controls.description: Disable the Remote Registry service to prevent remote read/write access to the registry over the network.
controls.rationale: Remote Registry exposes the registry to remote users/services; disabling reduces lateral-attack and information-disclosure opportunities.

os_applicability.notes: Disabling may affect remote management tools that rely on the Remote Registry service; provide alternate management mechanisms.

enforcement_methods.notes: Setting Start = 4 disables service. Alternatively enforce via Group Policy / service management templates.

validation_checks.command: reg query "HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\RemoteRegistry" /v Start & sc query RemoteRegistry
validation_checks.success_criteria: Start = 4 (disabled) and service state is STOPPED or disabled.

validation_checks.command: Attempt remote registry connect from a test host (e.g., reg.exe \<host> query HKLM) and verify access denied/unreachable.

failure_modes: Remote management/monitoring tools that rely on the service will fail; ensure alternate management (WMI/WinRM/agent) is available.
Some security products may rely on RemoteRegistry for inventory; include exceptions as required.

priority.priority_reason: Eliminates a remote attack surface; ensure management alternatives are ready.

references.title: How to disable Windows Remote Registry? — Microsoft Answers / documentation

controls.implementation_notes: Validate impact on remote management/backup agents. Use endpoint management agents (SCCM/Intune) or WinRM for remote maintenance where RemoteRegistry is disabled.

controls.name: Restrict anonymous access (RestrictAnonymous / RestrictAnonymousSAM / EveryoneIncludesAnonymous)
controls.description: Harden LSA anonymous access settings to prevent null-session enumeration of shares, SAM accounts and named pipes.
controls.rationale: Anonymous/null-session enumeration enables reconnaissance (user lists, shares) used by attackers for lateral movement; set LSA keys to restrict anonymous access.

os_applicability.notes: Applies broadly; test legacy apps that rely on anonymous access.

enforcement_methods.notes: Values: RestrictAnonymous=1 prevents null sessions from enumerating shares; RestrictAnonymousSAM=1 prevents SAM enumeration; EveryoneIncludesAnonymous=0 ensures anonymous does not get Everyone privileges.

validation_checks.command: reg query "HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa" /v RestrictAnonymous & reg query "HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa" /v RestrictAnonymousSAM & reg query "HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa" /v EveryoneIncludesAnonymous
validation_checks.success_criteria: RestrictAnonymous = 1, RestrictAnonymousSAM = 1, EveryoneIncludesAnonymous = 0 (or baseline values per policy).

validation_checks.command: Run controlled null-session enumeration tools in lab to verify SAM/share enumeration blocked.
validation_checks.success_criteria: Null-session enumeration attempts fail to enumerate users/shares.

failure_modes: Legacy apps or scripts that depend on anonymous enumeration will fail — coordinate with application owners and provide alternatives.
Not all null-session vectors are closed by these keys alone; combine with Network access policies and RestrictNullSessAccess on LanManServer.
controls.functional_impact: Medium — may break legacy discovery or monitoring relying on anonymous access.

priority.priority_reason: Prevents common reconnaissance techniques used in early-stage attacks.

audit_query_examples: Run controlled null-session enumeration test in lab

references.title: Network access: Do not allow anonymous enumeration of SAM accounts and shares (LSA keys guidance)
Practical notes on RestrictAnonymous usage and impacts

controls.implementation_notes: Roll out in stages; pilot on non-critical hosts and document exceptions. Combine with NET-09 RestrictNullSessAccess and SID/ACL reviews for full remediation.

source_references_loadbearing.claim: NoDriveTypeAutoRun values and 0xFF disables AutoPlay for all drives (but with caveats).
AlwaysInstallElevated behavior and security warning — must be 0 in HKLM and HKCU to be safe.
AutoShareWks / AutoShareServer registry keys control automatic creation of administrative shares (set to 0 to prevent creation).
AllowInsecureGuestAuth registry controls SMB insecure guest logons; disable (0) to prevent unauthenticated guest access.
RestrictAnonymous / RestrictAnonymousSAM / EveryoneIncludesAnonymous LSA keys limit null-session enumeration and anonymous access.
Disable RemoteRegistry by setting HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\RemoteRegistry Start = 4 and stopping the service.
